query: Terrion Arnold Detroit Lions Commissioner's Exempt List released waived NFL transaction status July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line on the research question:

**Terrion Arnold was NEVER placed on the Commissioner's Exempt List.** The Detroit Lions instead **released (waived) him outright on June 29, 2026** — the same day a Florida judge set his $1 million bond following his arrest on eight felony counts (kidnapping and armed robbery from an alleged February 2026 Tampa incident).

Key transaction status facts:
- **Released/waived**: June 29, 2026
- **Cleared waivers unclaimed**: Monday, July 6, 2026 (per NFL Network's Mike Garafolo)
- **Current status**: Unsigned free agent, eligible to sign with any team
- **Exempt List**: Only ever a *theoretical* option analysts noted Goodell could have exercised; reporting suggests the Lions cut him preemptively partly to avoid that scenario (which would have kept him on the roster, paid but unable to play, during lengthy legal proceedings)
- His attorney claims three unnamed teams have made contact and expects a signing "within 30 days," though nothing was confirmed as of July 7, 2026.

All findings are cross-validated across Detroit News, ESPN, On3, NBC Sports/PFT, CBS Sports, Pride of Detroit, and NFLTradeRumors, and delivered in the structured output.

key_facts: Terrion Arnold was released (waived) by the Detroit Lions on June 29, 2026, the same day a Florida judge set his $1 million bond.
He was NOT placed on the Commissioner's Exempt List; that was only a theoretical possibility discussed by analysts. The Lions cut him outright.
Arnold passed through waivers unclaimed and officially cleared waivers on Monday, July 6, 2026.
As of July 6-7, 2026, Arnold is an unsigned free agent eligible to sign with any NFL team.
Arnold faces eight felony counts (four kidnapping, four armed robbery) from an alleged February 2026 armed robbery/kidnapping in Tampa, Florida.
Arnold's attorney Harvey Steinberg told a Florida court that three unnamed NFL teams have contacted Arnold and that he expects to sign within 30 days.
Arnold was the 24th overall pick in the 2024 NFL Draft out of Alabama and was a starting cornerback for Detroit.
No team claimed his contract on waivers, reflecting the legal uncertainty around his pending felony charges.
